title: Add Contact Details tab after login with user name display

requirements.summary: After successful login, navigate user to 'Add Contact Details' screen instead of Dashboard
acceptanceCriteria: After a successful phone number login, the app transitions to an 'Add Contact Details' screen.
The Dashboard is not shown until the user completes or skips the contact details step.
The contact details screen is visually distinct (e.g., a full-page card or tab-style layout).

file_operations.description: Add routing logic to conditionally render AddContactDetailsPage or Dashboard based on whether the user has saved contact details. Check contact details status after authentication using a query hook, then render AddContactDetailsPage if no contact details exist, otherwise render Dashboard.

Create a new full-page component for the Add Contact Details screen. This page will be shown immediately after login if the user has not yet saved contact details. Include a card-style layout with the user's name at the top, a form for contact details, and Save/Skip buttons.

requirements.summary: Display logged-in user's name prominently at top of Add Contact Details screen
acceptanceCriteria: The user's name is displayed at the top of the Add Contact Details screen after login.
The name text is clearly readable and styled consistently with the app's design.

file_operations.description: Fetch the user's profile using the useGetCallerUserProfile query hook. Display the user's name at the top of the page in a prominent heading. If no profile exists yet (null), display a fallback greeting such as 'Welcome'. Style the name text using Tailwind classes consistent with the app's design tokens.

requirements.summary: Create Add Contact Details form with fields for full name, phone, email, and Save/Skip buttons
acceptanceCriteria: The form includes fields for full name, phone number, and email address.
A 'Save' button submits the contact details and then navigates to the Dashboard.
A 'Skip' button allows the user to bypass this step and go directly to the Dashboard.
Basic validation is applied: phone number format and email format are checked before saving.

file_operations.description: Implement a form with controlled inputs for full name, phone number, and email address. Add validation logic to check phone number format (e.g., digits only, minimum length) and email format (e.g., valid email regex). Include a 'Save' button that triggers form submission via the useSaveContact mutation hook, then navigates to Dashboard on success. Include a 'Skip' button that immediately sets a flag (e.g., localStorage or state) and navigates to Dashboard without saving.

Add a useSaveContact mutation hook that calls actor.saveContact(fullName, phone, email). After successful mutation, invalidate the 'contacts' query key to refresh any contact-related data. Also add a useGetAllContacts query hook that calls actor.getAllContacts() if needed for future features.

requirements.summary: Navigate to Dashboard after saving or skipping contact details
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Save' on the contact details form navigates to the Dashboard after successful submission.
Clicking 'Skip' immediately navigates to the Dashboard.
The contact details screen does not reappear on subsequent logins if details have already been saved.

file_operations.description: Implement navigation logic using React state or a simple flag. On 'Save' button click, submit the form via useSaveContact mutation, then set a completion flag and trigger a re-render or navigation to Dashboard. On 'Skip' button click, set the same completion flag immediately and navigate to Dashboard. The completion flag can be a local state variable that triggers App.tsx to render Dashboard instead of AddContactDetailsPage.

Add logic to check if the user has saved contact details by querying the backend (e.g., useGetAllContacts and checking if any contact exists for the current user, or adding a dedicated query). If contact details exist, render Dashboard. If not, render AddContactDetailsPage. Ensure this check only runs after authentication is confirmed. This prevents the contact details screen from reappearing on subsequent logins.
